BRIEF EF0 TORNADO TOUCHDOWN ABOUT 2.5 MILES SOUTH OF BELL CITY...NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 97 AND HWY 1270. THE ACTUAL TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED 0.2 MILES EAST OF HWY 97 ON HWY 1270. MINOR TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM THE TOUCHDOWN. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 75 MPH WITH A 40 YARD PATH WIDTH.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 464 NEAR ALMO AND TRAVELLED 3/4 MILE TO THE NORTHEAST BEFORE LIFTING. AVERAGE PATH WIDTH WAS 40 YARDS. PEAK WIND WAS 80 MPH. SEVERAL TREES OR TREE LIMBS WERE DOWNED. ONE LARGE OAK TREE WAS UPROOTED. MINOR DAMAGE WAS DONE TO SIDING ON A HOME. THERE WAS ONE EYEWITNESS OF THE TORNADO.

AN EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED FROM 3 MILES NORTHEAST OF ALMO KENTUCKY TO NEAR HICO KENTUCKY. PEAK WINDS WERE 75 MPH. AVERAGE PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH WAS 2 MILES. SEVERAL TREE LIMBS AND TREE TOPS WERE DOWNED AND SOME FIELD CORN WAS BLOWN OVER.

AN EFO TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF GOLDEN POND KENTUCKY TO 3.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF GOLDEN POND KENTUCKY. PEAK WIND WAS 70 MPH. AVERAGE PATH WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH WAS 0.5 MILES. SEVERAL TREE LIMBS AND TREE TOPS WERE DOWNED.
TREE LIMBS DOWNED AROUND CUMBERLAND SHORES...AS WELL AS IN WRANGLERS CAMPGROUND IN THE LAND BETWEEN THE LAKES.

EF1 TORNADO WAS NOTED 1.25 MILES SE OF HERNDON OR 11 MILES SSW OF HOPKINSVILLE TO 1.75 MILES SE HERNDON OR 11 MILES S OF HOPKINSVILLE. PEAK WIND WAS ESTIMATED AT 95 MPH. AVERAGE PATH WIDTH WAS 110 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH WAS 1/2 MILE. THE TORNADO CAUSED A FEW DOZEN TREES TO BE UPROOTED AND NUMEROUS BROKEN LARGE LIMBS. IN ADDITION...A SMALL AREA OF CORN WAS FLATTENED AND MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ON ONE HOME.

SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ALONG HWY 66 FROM HWY 261 TO LINCOLN AVE AND ALONG VANN RD BETWEEN HWY 261 AND HWY 61. WATER WAS UP TO VEHICLE DOORS ON HWY 66. THREE VEHICLES WERE STRANDED IN HIGH WATER ON HWY 261 JUST OUTSIDE NEWBURGH. ONE VEHICLE STRANDED AT INTERSECTION OF HWY 66/261.
